##NAMES=Redmondite
##RRUFFID=R250069
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=[Pb_8_O_2_Zn(OH)_6_](S_2_O_3_)_4_
##LOCALITY=Redmond mine, Haywood County, North Carolina, USA
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Colorless, transparent, equant rhombs sometimes showing varying shades of brown, possibly due to inclusions or underlying minerals. It occurs together with hydroredmondite (R250068) and  sulfatoredmondite (R250070) in a highly unusual secondary assemblage that comprises a variety of rare Pb-Zn-Cu sulfates, thiosulfates, and carbonates. Type sample, described in Kampf et al (2023)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250069
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=[Pb_7.99_O_2_Zn_1.01_(OH)_6_](S_2_O_3_)_3.68_(SO_4_)_0.32_
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 9.1672 b: 10.6576 c: 14.062 alpha: 90 beta: 101.173 gamma: 90 volume: 1347.83 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
